text.title: RAG Retrieval Rules
text.summary: Rules for retrieval strategy selection, hybrid search as default, reranking requirements, context budget management, and retrieved chunk ordering.

rule.statement: Use hybrid search (BM25 + vector) by default. Fall back to pure vector only after benchmarking shows no improvement.
rule.rationale: Pure semantic search misses exact technical terms, product codes, and rare names. Hybrid search adds BM25 coverage with minimal additional latency on most vector databases.

rule.statement: Use a cross-encoder reranker for all production retrieval. Retrieve top-20, rerank to top-5.
rule.rationale: Vector search similarity scores are calibrated for recall, not precision. A cross-encoder reranker scores candidate chunks against the full query, improving precision at the cost of 100–300ms latency.

rule.statement: Ensure top_k * avg_chunk_tokens stays well below the model's context limit after adding system prompt and few-shot examples.
rule.rationale: Context overflow causes the model to drop earlier chunks. The dropped chunks are typically the less similar ones, but they may contain the answer for complex queries.

rule.statement: Cache frequent queries at the application layer — same query string produces the same retrieval result.
rule.rationale: Embedding + retrieval adds 200–600ms. For repeated queries in high-traffic applications, caching eliminates this latency entirely.

rule.statement: Put highest-scored chunks first in the prompt context. Models attend better to earlier content.
rule.rationale: LLMs exhibit "lost in the middle" behavior: facts in the middle of a long context are attended to less than facts at the beginning or end. Highest-relevance chunks should be first.

example.code:
from llama_index.core import StorageContext, load_index_from_storage from llama_index.core.postprocessor import SentenceTransformerRerank
storage = StorageContext.from_defaults(persist_dir="./index") index = load_index_from_storage(storage)
reranker = SentenceTransformerRerank( model="cross-encoder/ms-marco-MiniLM-L-2-v2", top_n=5 # rerank 20 down to 5 )
engine = index.as_query_engine( similarity_top_k=20, node_postprocessors=[reranker] ) response = engine.query("your question here")
example.note: similarity_top_k=20 for broad recall; reranker narrows to top 5 for precision.
